title: New evangelization of Catholic Church
sub_topics: Third Philippine Conference on New Evangelization (PCNE III)
pro-God / pro-faith / pro-life / pro-prayer / pro-family / pro-human-rights criteria
the end never justifies the means

stances.claim: Separation of church and state does not prohibit clergy and laity from advocating pro-Christian tenets — it requires only government neutrality among denominations, prohibiting public funding for one religion or using religion as condition for civil rights.
stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal clarification + enumeration of what separation does/does not prohibit

stances.claim: There is separation of church from state, but no separation of state from God — the Philippines is theist (monotheist), not atheist or agnostic; the Constitution's preamble 'imploring the aid of Almighty God' and the prayers opening sessions of Congress, the Cabinet, and the Supreme Court demonstrate this.
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-theological distinction + textual evidence from Constitution and institutional practice

stances.claim: Catholics must bear witness to faith not only in internal life but also in political and social spheres — per Pope Francis's Evangelii Gaudium, 'it is no longer possible to claim that religion should be restricted to the private sphere.'
stances.rhetorical_move: papal authority + Vatican II + catechism citation

stances.claim: Charter change to federalism must be approached with vigilance — the method (Con-ass vs Con-con) is the first challenge; the second is making the new Constitution 'as pro-God, pro-faith, pro-life, pro-prayer, pro-family and pro-human rights as possible.'
stances.rhetorical_move: two-challenge framing + enumerative criteria for evaluation

stances.claim: The proposed revival of death penalty by hanging or musketry and summary execution of alleged criminals without due process must be opposed — 'the end never justifies the means.'

institutions: Philippine Conference on New Evangelization (PCNE III)
events: PCNE III at UST Quadricentennial Pavilion (July 15-17, 2016)

signature_phrases: there is separation of church from state, but no separation of the state from God
We, the sovereign Filipino people, imploring the aid of Almighty God
pro-God, pro-faith, pro-life, pro-prayer, pro-family and pro-human rights
workers in the vineyard of the Lord
the end never justifies the means
above us to inspire us, behind us to strengthen us, before us to lead us, and within us to transform us
register_markers: Separation of church and state. (section header)
decision_framework_signals: applies 'state neutral among religions, not neutral about God' framework as church-state interpretive principle
uses Pope-Francis-as-authority framing for Catholic public engagement
applies pro-God/faith/life/prayer/family/human-rights criteria as Charter-change evaluation rubric
uses 'end never justifies the means' as categorical moral limit on policy
notable_anecdotes: Cardinal Tagle inviting him to speak at PCNE III on how Catholics can evangelize in governance and public service
